comment: Launcher manifest (issue #226, scope item 2): the curated list of the 9 bundled Toys. Each entry's `id` is the instrument document name under instruments/<id>.json; `title`/`blurb` are player-facing (blurbs adapted from the README rig table); `kind` is self-playing (sounds on load) or tap-to-play (surfaces trigger widgets); `order` sets grid position. The staging script (scripts/stage-assets.mjs) stages exactly these Toys + their transitive resources into public/. Default Toy is groovebox (order 1) — its assets are prefetched at splash so the first pick is instant.

toys.title: Groovebox
toys.blurb: A free-running 16-step drum machine — kick, snare, and hat synthesized from operators on a shared clock. Toggle steps, ride the tempo.

toys.title: Vibrato
toys.blurb: A self-playing drone with an LFO sweeping the pitch. Tweak the rate, depth, and center.

toys.title: Sequence
toys.blurb: A clock-driven step melody — the sequencer walks an 8-step degree pattern into the synth.

toys.title: Scale Demo
toys.blurb: The same step melody resolved through a tonal context in C minor — change the key and it re-spells live.

toys.title: Sampler Arp
toys.blurb: A self-playing sample arpeggio — a clock-driven sequencer fires a blip through a major arpeggio.

toys.title: DJ Filter
toys.blurb: A self-playing saw arpeggio through a DJ-mixer filter knob — one bipolar control sweeps a low-pass down or a high-pass up.

toys.title: Metronome
toys.blurb: A click on every beat from the clock. Ride the tempo.

toys.title: Chord Player
toys.blurb: Tap and hold diatonic triad buttons (I–vii°). Chords re-spell live when you change key — a 12-voice pad.

toys.title: Strum Harp
toys.blurb: Drag to strum — the bar plucks a note each time it crosses a string. Strings stay in key through the tonal context.
